entry.title: A Candid Conversation with Erin Brooks
entry.content:
Erin Brooks, iPhoneographer extraordinaire, joins us for a far-reaching chat about modern photography. Along the way, we find out about her involvement in the Shot on iPhone campaign for Apple, her favorite apps for editing, and where a standard mirrorless camera fits into her workflow.

Erin is such an awesome guest. Trust me, you’re not going to want to miss this one.

entry.title: Candid Conversations: Adobe’s Josh Haftel
entry.content: This week’s show features Josh Haftel, product manager for Lightroom Mobile at Adobe. He joins us in a wide-reaching discussion about the state of mobile photography, the future of imaging technology, and where Lightroom fits into the picture. Tons of really cool stuff to unpack in this one.

entry.title: Candid, Episode #40: Medium Format—The Bigger Picture
entry.content: Leica and Fuji have both announced new products, including a competitively priced medium format system, the GFX. We take this opportunity to talk about Medium Format as a whole, and who might be most tempted to make the leap.

entry.title: Candid, Episode #39: Everything Old is New Again... Except Lightroom
entry.content:
This week Marius and I do our best to catch up on the deluge of photo news from the week. The Panasonic GH5, Kodak’s Ektachrome revival, and DJI’s supposed transition to Hasselblad ownership are all addressed, along with a wishlist for Adobe Lightroom in 2017.

Also, don’t forget to join the conversation in our new public Slack channel! We already have a great community, and it’s growing every day. See you there!

entry.title: A review of the Think Tank Retrospective 6 camera bag
entry.content:
My last review of 2016 was about one of the best camera bags I’ve ever used: the Think Tank Retrospective 6.

Now, long-time readers may know that I’ve owned the smaller Retrospective 5 for a few years already. That means I knew what to expect from the new bag going in, and I’m happy to say it didn’t disappoint.

The Retrospective 6 has all of the strengths of the 5, but its larger size makes it a lot more versatile. I can easily carry a full kit with it, and the entire package is somehow still light enough on my shoulder that I don’t get tired, unlike my previous experience with the leather Brixton by ONA.

The Retrospective 6 has quickly become my go-to bag, both for everyday use and for travel. That’s no small feat, I assure you. If you want to know more about it, head on over to Tools & Toys for the full review.
